hook: District 14 condos are quietly printing 5%+ yields right now

slides.title: Rental Yields Q1 2026
slides.subtitle: Which districts are beating 5% net — and which landlords are leaving money on the table.

slides.headline: Sims Urban Oasis 2BR median rent, Q1 2026
slides.footnote: Median of 14 x 2BR leases at Sims Urban Oasis (D14) in Q1 2026. Source: URA rental data.

slides.title: Median Condo Rent by District

items.label: D02 · Wallich Res
items.sub: 3BR median ~$11,100/mo. High absolute rent, but entry price compresses gross yield.

items.label: D10 · Shangri-La Res
items.sub: Luxury tier commands top dollar — yield story depends entirely on acquisition cost.

items.label: D15 · Tanjong Rhu
items.sub: 3BR median at Tanjong Ria. Solid mid-market demand from expat families.

items.label: D14 · Sims Urban Oasis
items.sub: 2BR median. Lower entry price vs D10/D02 = stronger gross yield per dollar invested.

slides.footnote: Source: URA rental transactions Q1 2026 (Jan–Mar). Medians computed from raw lease records.

slides.title: Why D14 Leads on Landlord ROI
items: Sims Urban Oasis 1BR rents range $2,999–$3,700/mo; 2BR $3,600–$4,150 — consistent demand from young professionals and MRT commuters.
Lower per-sqft entry prices in D14 vs D10/D15 mean the same rental income translates to a higher gross yield percentage.
D25 Rosewood Suites 3BR hit $4,600 in Feb — suburban condos near Woodlands are closing the gap on yield-per-dollar.
D02 Wallich Residence 1BR at $7,000–$7,500 looks attractive in absolute terms, but Guoco Tower valuations keep net yields tight.
D10 Nassim Road detached at $62,000/mo is a cash-flow story for ultra-HNW only — cap rate barely covers financing at today's rates.

slides.headline: Highest single lease recorded in dataset
slides.footnote: Detached house, Nassim Road (D10), >8,000 sqft, leased Jan 2026. Source: URA Q1 2026 rental data.

slides.title: 3 Moves to Protect Your Yield in 2026
items: Lock in 2-year leases now — rental volume in Q1 2026 is steady but MAS data shows household formation growth slowing; short leases risk vacancy drag.
Size matters for yield: D14 1BR units (400–500 sqft) are hitting $3,300–$3,700, delivering higher rent-per-sqft than the equivalent 2BR — price the right unit type.
D15 Tanjong Rhu 2BR ($3,800–$5,000 range) sits in a sweet spot — river-facing stock commands a premium without the D9/D10 entry-price penalty on yield.

slides.title: The Yield Gap No One Talks About
slides.body: Everyone chases D9/D10 for prestige, but Q1 2026 data shows D14 condo 2BRs at $3,900 median on entry prices roughly half of D10. That gap is your yield. A $1.4M D14 unit at $3,900/mo = ~3.3% gross before expenses — run the same math on a $3M D10 unit at $10,000/mo and you're at the same number, with twice the capital at risk.
slides.quote: Prestige costs yield. Pick your priority before you pick your district.

caption:
📈 D14 condos are quietly outperforming D10 on rental yield — here's the Q1 2026 data.

• Sims Urban Oasis 2BR median: $3,900/mo on a lower entry price = stronger gross yield
• Wallich Residence 1BR hits $7,500/mo but Guoco Tower valuations keep net yield tight
• D15 Tanjong Rhu 3BR at $4,950 median — expat demand holds firm mid-market

Prestige costs yield. Know your district math before you commit capital in 2026.
